title: Traditional Portuguese Paella
description: A classic Spanish-inspired dish from Portugal, made with savory chicken, seafood, and spices.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and pork, cooking until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the meat from the pan, and add the shrimp, cooking until pink, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the sausage and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the pan, cooking until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the tomato paste, cooking for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the paella rice to the pan, stirring to coat with the oil and mix with the vegetables.
Add the chicken broth to the pan, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is tender.
Remove the paella from the heat, and let rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Note: Be sure to cook the paella over high heat to achieve the crispy crust at the bottom, known as the socarrat.
